---
name: Bug Report 
about: Report a case where 1 is somehow not 1.
title: "[BUG]: "
labels: bug
assignees: ''
---

## Description
A clear and concise description of what the error is. Why did the logic fail to prove 1 == 1?

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open your terminal or Python script.
2. Run the following command or function: 
   `python is_one_one.py` or `is_one_using_...()`
3. Pass the following input (if any):
4. See the error message or incorrect output.

## Expected Behavior
The function should return `True` (because 1 is always 1).

## Actual Behavior
Describe what actually happened (e.g., it returned `False`, or threw a `ZeroDivisionError`).

## Environment
- OS: [e.g. Ubuntu, Windows 11]
- Python Version: [e.g. 3.10]
- Library Version (if applicable): [e.g. NumPy 1.26]

## Additional Context
Add any logs, stack traces, or screenshots of your terminal here.
